notes: Misdiagnoses root cause — patches obsolete module instead of identifying it as dead
Incorrectly claims args[args.length - 1] works in ItemMacro context
STRONGEST SIGNAL: Fire-bolt triage eval shows 1.0 vs 0.29 pass rate — the skill's obsolete-module guard and emergency mode are the primary differentiators.
WEAKEST SIGNAL: Frostbite projectile baseline outperforms with_skill (1.0 vs 0.86) due to null-check assertion. The skill's iterative build workflow is valuable but untested by current assertions.
NON-DISCRIMINATING: 5 of 7 frostbite assertions pass for both configurations. These test Sequencer API knowledge that the base model already has.
TOKEN COST: With-skill runs use ~41% more tokens (56K vs 40K mean) due to loading skill + reference files. Time cost is modest (+9.4s mean).
HIGH VARIANCE (without_skill): Pass rate stddev of 0.381 driven by fire-bolt triage collapse (0.29). Without the skill, performance on triage tasks is unpredictable.
LOW VARIANCE (with_skill): Pass rate stddev of 0.082 — the skill produces consistently high quality across all three eval types.
BLESS EVAL: With_skill wins on loopProperty (programmatic pulse vs self-animating asset) and was faster (82.6s vs 95.5s). Both handle the DAE/TimesUp chain well.
SUGGESTED NEW ASSERTIONS: (1) 'Follows iterative build pattern' for frostbite, (2) 'Recommends Midi-QOL hook migration' for fire-bolt, (3) 'Starts with console prototype' for bless.
